title: ¿Por qué el precio de la vivienda social crece a mayor ritmo?
summary: El precio de la vivienda social en México aumentó 11% anual en el primer trimestre de 2026, el mayor incremento entre los segmentos habitacionales y por encima de la vivienda media y residencial. La escasez de oferta, el encarecimiento del suelo y los costos de materiales y financiamiento presionan el precio final. Este entorno complica el acceso a la vivienda para los segmentos de menores ingresos, representando un reto estructural para el mercado hipotecario en México.

title: Can USMCA Survive Trump's Second Term?
summary: Former diplomats and trade negotiators from Canada, Mexico, and the US agree that the USMCA review extends far beyond tariffs, underpinning supply chains, agriculture, intellectual property, and investment across North America. Key disputes remain over autos, China's role in supply chains, and industrial policy, while all parties stress the need to deepen regional economic integration. The broader question is whether North America can adapt to geopolitical pressures without unraveling a pact now deeply embedded in regional economies.

title: Hawkish Fed Throws Down Challenge for Emerging-Market Bond Rally
summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's hawkish stance has disrupted the emerging-market bond rally just as falling energy prices had offered some relief. Higher-for-longer US rates tighten financial conditions globally, raising borrowing costs for EM economies including Mexico. This complicates the outlook for Mexican fixed-income markets and could increase hedging and refinancing costs for financial institutions exposed to dollar-rate volatility.

title: Austria Lobbies EU to Host Anthropic After US Access Curbs
summary: Austria is pushing the EU to consider hosting Anthropic within its borders after the US restricted foreign access to its most advanced AI models. The move highlights how US AI export controls are reshaping the global competitive landscape for frontier AI, prompting European governments to seek domestic anchors for leading providers. The geopolitical fragmentation of AI access could accelerate EU-based AI investment and drive regulatory divergence from the US framework.

title: Why the US Needs its Neighbors for Energy
summary: Roughly 70% of Mexico's energy comes from the United States via natural gas, while Canada supplies about 60% of US oil imports, making North American energy an interconnected bilateral rather than trilateral system. Energy executives and regulators broadly agree the current arrangement functions well and should largely be preserved during USMCA negotiations. For Mexico, continued access to affordable US natural gas is critical to powering nearshoring manufacturing and overall economic competitiveness.

title: Desde US$30.000 por noche: así es OKA, la villa más lujosa de Brasil
summary: OKA, una villa privada en las colinas de Itacaré, Brasil, opera bajo un modelo todo incluido con estancias desde US$30,000 por noche y ocho suites de 45 metros cuadrados cada una. Desarrollada por el grupo sueco-brasileño Barracuda, se posiciona como la propiedad más lujosa de Brasil con chef 24/7, cava de vinos y arquitectura de autor en entorno natural. El crecimiento de este tipo de hospedaje ultra-premium refleja una tendencia en el turismo de lujo hacia experiencias totalmente curadas que eliminan los cargos adicionales.

title: Robots, not chatbots, will realise AI's potential
summary: Factory-floor robotics applications of AI, rather than conversational chatbots, could deliver the transformational productivity gains needed to boost rich-world economies, according to the Financial Times. Industrial AI enables more predictable, measurable returns than language models, which still struggle to demonstrate ROI at enterprise scale. This perspective challenges current generative AI hype while positioning physical AI as the more compelling long-term investment and productivity lever.

title: Europe's Hunt for AI Stocks Leads to Power Suppliers and Banks
summary: European investors seeking AI exposure are turning to enabling infrastructure — particularly power companies and banks — as valuations for traditional AI-adjacent tech stocks become stretched. Banks are viewed as AI beneficiaries through productivity gains and client-facing automation, while energy utilities are needed to power the data center buildout. The rotation signals that AI's investment thesis is broadening beyond pure-play tech into traditional industrial and financial sectors.

title: Google Caps Meta's Use of Gemini AI, Financial Times Reports
summary: Google has placed limits on Meta's access to its Gemini AI models because it could not provide the computing capacity Meta needed, highlighting how surging AI demand is turning compute into a scarce commodity. The constraints create bottlenecks even for the largest tech buyers and reshape commercial relationships between frontier AI providers. For enterprises evaluating AI strategies, the compute crunch reinforces the case for diversifying model providers rather than concentrating on a single supplier.

title: AI 'exuberance' risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns
summary: The Bank for International Settlements warned that overly optimistic AI investment expectations risk triggering a sharp pullback in tech funding, with potential ripple effects across global credit markets. Weak returns on AI infrastructure could undermine the financial stability of firms that have levered up to finance data centers and compute capacity. The BIS flagged AI exuberance alongside inflation and fiscal stress as among the most pressing threats to global financial stability.

title: AI Bust Risks Ripple Effects From Growth to Credit, BIS Says
summary: The BIS 2026 Annual Economic Report flagged an AI investment bust, persistent inflation, and fiscal stress as the three most alarming near-term threats to global prosperity. A correction in AI-related spending could propagate through credit channels, dampening growth for economies with significant exposure to the AI capital cycle. The warning arrives as central banks face the dual challenge of managing inflation while monitoring emerging financial stability risks from AI-driven leverage.

title: OpenAI Hires Uber's Prabhjeet Singh to Lead India Operations
summary: OpenAI appointed Prabhjeet Singh, formerly Uber's India and South Asia president, as managing director for India — one of its fastest-growing markets globally. The hire signals OpenAI's commitment to deepening its emerging-market footprint as competition for AI users and enterprise clients intensifies. India represents a major growth frontier for AI adoption across financial services, software, and consumer applications.

title: Google caps Meta's Gemini use as AI demand strains capacity
summary: Google limited Meta's use of Gemini AI models as surging demand for advanced AI outpaces available computing capacity, with the constraint underscoring how compute scarcity is now the primary bottleneck in AI deployment. The dynamic is reshaping commercial relationships between tech giants and frontier model providers, as even the best-resourced companies cannot guarantee supply. For enterprises planning AI rollouts, the episode reinforces the need to hedge across multiple model vendors and infrastructure providers.

title: Bond giant Pimco flexes muscles with private placements push
summary: Pimco is aggressively expanding into private placements, targeting borrowers who need capital outside public markets as the boundary between public and private credit continues to blur. The move reflects the broader trend of asset managers competing directly with banks in private credit, driven by institutional demand for higher-yielding alternatives. Pimco's scale and distribution give it a competitive edge in what is becoming the most contested growth arena in global capital markets.

title: AI Fever Powers HK Share Sales Through Hurdles to Five-Year High
summary: Hong Kong equity share sales hit a five-year high in H1 2026, fueled by investor enthusiasm for AI-related listings that overcame regulatory headwinds and a sluggish equity market. AI companies and AI-adjacent infrastructure providers dominated the strongest IPO pipeline Hong Kong has seen since 2021. The trend illustrates how AI is reshaping capital markets activity across Asia, with implications for global EM investment flows.

title: La IA y el nearshoring elevarán 10% la demanda eléctrica, pero el capital llega lento
summary: La combinación de inteligencia artificial y nearshoring podría elevar hasta 10% la demanda eléctrica en México, pero el financiamiento para la transición energética avanza a un ritmo insuficiente para cubrir esa necesidad. La escasez de capital retrasa proyectos clave de generación y transmisión, comprometiendo la competitividad del país como destino manufacturero. Este déficit representa tanto un riesgo operativo para empresas en México como una oportunidad de inversión en infraestructura energética.

title: AI's Energy Crunch Has Investors Searching for Next IPO Winners
summary: Wall Street is pouring billions into energy companies that promise to solve AI's power consumption problem, even as some of the enabling technology remains unproven. The AI energy buildout is generating a new wave of IPO candidates in power generation, grid management, and data center cooling, attracting both strategic and financial investors. The capital cycle mirrors earlier tech infrastructure booms, raising questions about valuation discipline and return timelines.

title: Anthropic's Mythos 5 AI Model Cleared by US for Wider Use
summary: Anthropic's Mythos 5 AI model has received US government approval for use by trusted international partners, after earlier being restricted over national security concerns. The clearance represents a significant regulatory milestone for Anthropic and signals that the US is developing a tiered framework for governing foreign access to frontier AI models. This development directly intersects with the EU-Anthropic hosting discussions and sets a precedent for how powerful AI will be governed and commercialized globally.

title: Forum AI CEO on Pitfalls With AI in Politics
summary: Forum AI's co-founder Campbell Brown argued that AI companies must open themselves to greater scrutiny to prevent misuse in political contexts. The discussion highlights the growing governance gap between AI capabilities and institutional oversight, particularly as AI tools are increasingly deployed in information environments. Brown's call for transparency from AI providers aligns with broader regulatory momentum toward mandated disclosure and accountability frameworks.
